sections.title: Talking to Patzi after rejecting the quest

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Hello again! Are you feeling more in the partying spirit now?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Good day! I don't suppose I could trouble you for the briefest of moments?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I could consider being troubled for a little...

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Ah, on second thoughts, you may not be cut out for this job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you! I could consider being troubled, for the right reasons.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: How does access to a rather fancy party sound as a motivator?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, just as long as I get to actually enjo-

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I'm sure I can also rustle up some payment to encourange you, if you have any reservations.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You may even find some... exciting opportunities inside, given the presence of some of Aldarin's wealthiest citizens!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if you're going to this party, would you also be one of Aldarin's wealthiest citizens?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Alas, I am, quite unfortunately, not rich in money. What I do possess is a certain wealth...

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: ... of talent! For you see I am... an actor! Patzi the Grand, they'll call me... eventually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They call me [player name]... just [player name]. So if you're not rich in money, how am I going to get paid, assuming I agree to help you with... whatever it is you want.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Cutting words, but you need not worry. While you won't be getting paid upfront, I have a feeling that if all goes well, I will soon find myself with more than enough wealth to handsomely reward you.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: And besides, if nothing else, I'm sure you'll have a wonderful time at the party!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I could do with letting my hair down a little. Alright, I'm in.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So whose party are we going to?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Well, that's complicated...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't know?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Sometimes a man has to follow his heart... and a second hand invitation saying there's a party at Villa Lucens tonight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Villa Lucens?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Yes! The villa is home to an almighty patron of the arts! He even has his own theatre atop the cliffs behind the villa! In fact, the whole reason for this party is because he's showing his new play!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And yet you don't actually know who he is?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: This opportunity came about quite suddenly. As such, I'm unfortunately not as prepared as I otherwise would be. However, an actor like myself can't turn down the chance to meet with a man like him.

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Not to mention the chance to enjoy a good party.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Well said, Adala. Oh that reminds me, [player name], this is Adala. She's also helping me out today!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you have her, why do you need me?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: She advertised herself as a bag carrier, so she's here to move my bag around. There's a lot of costumes and props that I travel with, so it's nice to have someone else carry them.

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I'm not going to say no to going to a fancy party, even as the help.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: As for your part in all this, [player name], with this being a fancy party and all, I'm going to need a butler to help me fit in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A butler?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You know, serving me wine and tending to my needs. Fancy suit and all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So I don't actually get to enjoy the party at all, do I?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Oh I'm sure you will. You'll just have to act a little here and there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... You mentioned a fancy suit. You wouldn't happen to have a butler outfit in that bag you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I don't believe I do. I don't really take roles like that these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, in that case, any idea where I might get one, preferably free of charge?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Lots of the villas around here will have butlers, and butlers mean uniforms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you suggesting I steal one?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I suppose you could always put it back once you're done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I'm sure whoever I take it from will be overjoyed to have had someone else wearing their uniform.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Hmm... Maybe I should add a butler's uniform to my vast array of costumes after all.

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I doubt they'll ever even realise. Anyway, I remember seeing some uniforms through a window on the way over here. They were in a villa to the north.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... I guess I'm off to find a uniform then.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not really a party person these days, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Quite unfortunate. In that case, please make sure to keep an eye out for me at your local theatre. I'll be a star soon!

sections.title: Talking to Patzi without a uniform

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: How's the costume hunt going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I'm going to be working, shouldn't we be calling it a uniform?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Yes, I suppose so! Either way, Adala should be able to help you get your hands on one.

sections.title: Talking to Adala without a uniform

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Any luck finding that villa with the spare uniforms in yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite. You said it was to the north of here?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I believe so. Once you get there, you should keep an eye out for any guards. Wouldn't want you to get arrested before the party starts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This person has a lot of uniforms, but I shouldn't take more than I need.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the house window while in view of the guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I probably shouldn't break into a house in full view of a guard.

lines.speaker: Wandering Guard
lines.text: I definitely wouldn't recommend it.

sections.title: Talking to Adala or Patzi with a uniform

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I see you've managed to find a uniform.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have indeed. You must have a good eye to have seen them through a window as you walked by?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: It was a bit of a lucky spot, I must admit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lucky indeed. It's almost like you were specifically looking for them?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Just good fortune. Now, I ought to get a move on getting this bag inside.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Oh, she's in a rush, isn't she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Given the size of your bag, I'm not sure she's rushing anywhere.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Well, she sets a good example. You should probably be heading on in yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing the entrance is just up these stairs?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: The guest entrance is yes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't tell me...

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You'll have to go in through the staff entrance, unfortunately. I can't be seen entering with the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The help?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Sorry, just getting into character.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You will have to use the staff entrance though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find said entrance?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I'll bet Adala went through it. You should've followed her! I think she headed somewhere along that hedgerow.

sections.title: Talking to Patzi after Adala is gone

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Oh, she's in a rush, isn't she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Given the size of your bag, I'm not sure she's rushing anywhere.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Well, she sets a good example. You should probably be heading on in yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing the entrance is just up these stairs?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: The guest entrance is yes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't tell me...

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You'll have to go in through the staff entrance, unfortunately. I can't be seen entering with the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The help?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Sorry, just getting into character.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You will have to use the staff entrance though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find said entrance?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I'll bet Adala went through it. You should've followed her! I think she headed somewhere along that hedgerow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where was that staff entrance that you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I'm guessing Adala went through it. I think she headed somewhere along that hedgerow.

sections.title: Attempting to enter through the guest entrance

lines.speaker: Villa Guard
lines.text: Sorry, entry here is for guests only.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, just give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Nilsal to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'm here to work.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Not without a uniform you're not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, just give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Good good. We'll hold on to any items you have on you whilst you're in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Then you should put your follower away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Oh, nilsal, to you. I wasn't expecting anyone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, there was a bit of a hold up. I'm quite new to this.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: A hold up? Nothing bad I hope? I've known people to have problems with their employers before. Is yours treating you well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems... friendly enough. I'm still not sure if I'm getting paid though.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Tetamo! Oh dear, oh dear. Are you part of a union?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't remember joining one. I probably should with the... varied working conditions I go through.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: We have a fantastic one here on Aldarin, if you're interested. I can't say discussions are effective, but sometimes the wealthy do come to the table!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think this is a one-time gig for me, but thank you!

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Very well. In that case, there are just a few quick admin bits to go through before I let you in.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: First and foremost, butlers must keep their uniforms and masks on at all times. If you are seen without yours, you will be ejected from the party.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got it. No taking off my uniform. What's this about masks?

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: All staff are to be as anonymous as possible tonight so we'll provide you with a mask on entry. We'll be using them to keep track of who's coming and going.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Now, for security purposes, butlers are required to enter and leave with completely empty pockets. We'll hold on to anything you need us to whilst you're in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got it, nothing in and nothing out.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Right, I think that covers everything. Are you ready to enter?

lines.speaker: Villa Guard
lines.text: Sorry, you'll need to use the staff exit.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Did you want to leave?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

sections.title: Talking to the head butler again

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Ah, welcome back. Are you ready to enter?

sections.title: Talking to the head butler after having entered the Villa

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Do you want to go back in?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

sections.title: Attempting to speak to anyone but Patzli

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably speak to Patzi before doing anything in here.

lines.speaker: Villa Guard
lines.text: Sorry, entry here is for guests only.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the 2nd floor[UK]3rd floor[US]

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shouldn't go too far from the party for now.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: How's your little task going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't quite made my way around everyone the party yet.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You made it in, and don't you look the part! Magnificent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel like a court jester...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel like a court jester... and not for the first time either.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Nonsense! Now, why not go and introduce yourself to our host and the other guests? I could do with knowing a little more about everyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on, I'm meant to be an anonymous butler. How do you expect me to get to know people for you?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I'm sure you can get their names at least. That will give me something to work with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... Anything else I can do for you, especially things you could easily do for yourself?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: No, no. I think you're doing more than enough right now, thank you.

sections.title: Talking to Constantinius again (house not on Aldarin)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, Constantinius, how often do you put on these parties?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Whenever we have a new play to unveil! I've got a couple of creatives from Tempestus that can write up a storm, so I find myself hosting these events more and more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does one usually get themselves invited?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Normally we just send the invitations out to all the villas on the island! If you can afford one, then you're welcome here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm actually wealthy enough to be a homeowner myself, but not on Aldarin unfortunately.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: You must try to change that before next time. After all, the more the merrier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you're definitely okay with me and my... we'll call him my employer, I guess. You're definitely okay with us being here?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Well, we sadly had a few who couldn't attend this time, so I'm actually quite glad you're here. Still, I wouldn't mind knowing how your employer managed to acquire an invitation...

sections.title: Talking to Constantinius again (house on Aldarin)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, Constantinius, how often do you put on these parties?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Whenever we have a new play to unveil! I've got a couple of creatives from Tempestus that can write up a storm, so I find myself hosting these events more and more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does one usually get themselves invited?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Normally we just send the invitations out to all the villas on the island! If you can afford one, then you're welcome here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I actually own a home here on the island. Perhaps I can be invited next time!

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Perhaps so. After all, the more the merrier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you're definitely okay with me and my... we'll call him my employer, I guess. You're definitely okay with us being here?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Well, we sadly had a few who couldn't attend this time, so I'm actually quite glad you're here. Still, I wouldn't mind knowing how your employer managed to acquire an invitation...

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: So, this is grand isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It certainly seems to be quite the event.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I'd say it's one of my finest yet, though I do wish more of the guests could've made it. However, it looks like we've gained a bit of a straggler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A straggler?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Yes, that slightly pompous fellow that hasn't introduced himself to anyone yet. The fact that he's here means he must have gained an invitation, though I don't know how...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Is that going to be an issue?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I'm sure it won't be! I'm always open to meeting new people, especially those who enjoy the arts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh good, because it's actually him who I'm here with. He was very keen to meet you, even though he didn't actually know your name...

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I can't believe it! How could he not even know the name of his gracious host?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps he's new to the island... Still, for an actor, his research seems lacking.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: He's an actor you say? Ah, now that explains why he was so eager to be here. Well, if he's good, this might be his lucky day. I, Constantinius, am in the market for a new actor to sponsor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sponsor?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Well, you know actors. Very profitable once they're popular. I like to... make investments.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: For example, I've sponsored tonight's lead actor, Livius, for some time. He's done very well for himself, and for me, though he's almost too expensive for me these days.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Anyway, perhaps you'll see him tonight on stage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still enjoying yourself, Cozyac?

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: Absolutely! Though I'm starting to feel myself in need of another drink. I don't suppose you could sort that when you have a moment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can certainly look into it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, sir.

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: Sir? I thought I'd left that behind when I retired. Please, call me Cozyac.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely, Cozyac. You can call me...

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: I seem to remember one of the rules of this party was that the staff were to remain anonymous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't call me then. Are you enjoying the party?

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: I am, yes. Though I am far more excited for tonight's performance. Constantinius says he's hired some of the finest actors for this one!

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Excuse me! You there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there. How can I be of service?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: I could do with... another drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a preference? Wine, or something non- alcoholic?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Alcoholic? I'll have you know that I, Pavo, am most certainly not an alcoholic. Now scram.

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Did you get that drink I asked for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sent me away before telling me what drink you actually wanted.

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: That's my mistake. I'll take some wine... Preferably a barrel of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Nilsal to you as well. You seem different to the other staff here. First time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is. How could you tell?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: You see similar things on boats. People look a bit wobbly until they settle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I hadn't noticed I was...

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Few ever do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you spend a lot of time on boats?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: I run something of a shipping company. If you need it moving, Xocotla's Got Ya!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't typically find myself in need of that sort of thing, but if that ever changes, I'll keep you in mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So about that shipping...

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: I'd have preferred not to talk about work, but I suppose you do seem friendly enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of things can you move?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: We can securely move pretty much anything. Animals, valuables, bulk cargo.

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Swiftness and discretion are valuable to us, if you catch my drift.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the cellar before being asked to

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: : I have no reason to go down there right now.

sections.title: Talking to Patzi after talking to everyone else

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I saw you make your way around the party! So then, who's who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, we've got...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pavo, the drunk one over there. I'm not sure there's much to them other than their love of drinking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xocotla, who seems a lot more relaxed. It seems that she runs a shipping business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Last of all for the guests we have Cozyac. He's quite up on the rules of this party, so he might not be happy that you weren't properly invited.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Pfft, invited? I have an invitation and I can act like I belong here! He'll never know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He might not, but our host Constantinius does.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Ah...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Luckily, he didn't actually seem to mind you being here too much. Apparently he's actually looking for a new actor to sponsor.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Sponsoring... me? How marvellous! Oh I think I should have a drink to celebrate! Could you be a dear and go into the wine cellar for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you like from the wine cellar? A beer?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Whilst that sounds lovely, I've heard there's some Principum Red down there! It's not produced any more so is rather hard to find, but our generous host seems happy enough to share.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, one glass of wine coming right up.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Did you manage to find that wine yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't had a chance to even go into the cellar yet. You'll have to give me a moment.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Remember, you're looking for Principum Red. I'm sure it will be labelled.

sections.title: Attempting to climb the stairs back up

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shouldn't go too far from the party for now.

sections.title: Attempting to open the door to the cupboard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Cupboard, keep out!' Why does it say keep out?

sections.title: Attempting to climb the stairs before investigating the wine

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shouldn't return to the party empty handed.

sections.title: Attempting to open the fancy chest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pri...pum Re...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... The label's faded, but I'm sure this is the wine Patzi was talking about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What... Oh, that's going to stain isn't it...

sections.title: Attempting to investigate the antique wine again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's probably best if I don't mess around with this again.

sections.title: Attempting to climb the stairs after investigating the wine

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I shouldn't leave without investigating that noise.

sections.title: Investigating the man on the floor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you had too much to drink?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You there! Don't move!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: You're coming with us.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: It's always the butler, isn't it.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Now now, Stradius. We don't know for sure they did it. It looks likely though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did what exactly?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Murder!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Stradius!

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: It was my turn to level the accusation at a suspect...

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: How often do we get to handle a murder?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: You have to respect the systems we have, Stradius!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still here, in case you forgot?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: So, murder. Interesting choice at a party full of wealthy people, wouldn't you say?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Poor Livius... He wasn't acting this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I didn't kill anyone?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You just happened to be found standing in a closed room with a dead body?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: With bloodstains on your clothes, no less.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bloodstains?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The red stains on your uniform. The victim's blood, I'm sure of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You found me in a wine cellar and you won't even consider that it might be wine?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I suppose I could try tasting it...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Stradius! What if it's blood?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Well, then it won't taste like wine, will it?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: No, you make a good point actually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just going to go...

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Oh no you won't! You're going to wait right there and answer some questions!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you believe me if I said I didn't do it?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: We'll consider it.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: We will?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: But first, questions! Why were you down in that cellar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was looking for a wine. Principum Red? Patzi sent me to get some for him to drink. I just happened to hear a commotion and thought someone may have been hurt.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: So you were just trying to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Honestly, I'm sold.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Stradius...

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Ah, yes, sorry...

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I'm afraid we can't just let you go. Not without an investigation.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Not to mention, given our whole job here was to stop anything bad happening at this party, we're not really doing so well right now. We'll look even more ineffective if we don't have a suspect...

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Could be a career ender, really.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Quite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... Well, much like I didn't kill that man, I suppose I don't want to hurt your careers. Maybe I could catch the killer!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Do you have any relevant qualifications?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Wait... are we meant to have...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: After all, training to be a butler must have taken a long time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm not even a butler! I got hired to act like one!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Right, now I have to put murder back on the table! Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just [player name]. I got hired by Patzi to be a butler for this evening, alongside a lady called Adala.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Adala? Do we know an Adala?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Yes, I believe she's accounted for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You ran into her?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: She came in shortly after we arrested you. Your friend, Patzi, mentioned she'd been sorting out some of his costumes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean she was only accounted for after the murder took place?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Why do I get the feeling we're not doing a good job of actually investigating this?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: It isn't just you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again, maybe I could be an extra pair of hands here.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Have a potential suspect investigate the murder? It wouldn't be orthodox, but it might work...

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I'm not sure I like the implication that we can't solve it ourselves.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: I agree, but we're not actually very qualified for this. We normally just guard drunk people, not solve murders!

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You make a fair point, Hutza.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: [player name], if you promise not to leave, can you help us work out what's happened here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... promise not to flee the scene?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Perfect! Shall we get to investigating then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where should I start?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The victim was Livius, the lead actor in tonight's play. His body is still down in the cellar, so you should probably go and inspect it, along with the rest of the crime scene.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: No doubt you'll also want to question everyone. We'll keep them busy for you until you're ready. We'll also give you a case file to track your notes in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, let's get to it.

sections.title: Attempting to open the room's door before finishing the conversation

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: And where do you think you're going?

sections.title: Talking to Hutza or Stradius after interrupting conversation

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I'm afraid we can't just let you go. Not without an investigation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you give me any tips for this investigation?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: You'll want to have a look down in the wine cellar I imagine. We made sure that nobody went down there after we arrested you, so everything should be as it was when the murder occurred.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You'll probably also want to do a bit of questioning up here as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you going to help with any of this?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: We'll keep an eye on everyone for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if the actual murderer is still in the cellar somewhere?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: We'll come looking for you if you're gone too long. It's not like there's anywhere for them to escape to!

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You will probably be dead by then though...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: On the bright side, it would prove your innocence.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Oh, this is exciting isn't it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I got arrested for murder because you sent me into that cellar!

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Did you at least manage to find what I sent you down there for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Principum Red? Of course. Constantinius has a nice large container of it. However, I'm not sure if that's the most...

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: See Adala, you were right!

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: What self-respecting man would have a wine cellar without such a historic wine in it?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: It's a shame we're locked down up here. I'd love to try it! I came so close too, if it weren't for that actor...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you blaming Livius for his own murder?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Of course not! I'm just ruing a missed chance to try that wine...

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Oh poor Livius! Where am I to find another actor of his calibre?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was he by any chance the one you mentioned earlier? The one who was starting to get too expensive for you?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I may have had some quibbles with how expensive his performances were, but he always made a profit!

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I can only hope his understudy is capable of filling the gap that's going to be left. Livius always kept her in the shadows, though he did say that she was nearly ready for a leading role of her own...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't happen to know Livius, did you Cozyac?

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: I'm familiar with his work, but not the man himself. He's been Constantinius' preferred actor for a few years now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did he ever come and join the parties? Perhaps you crossed paths during the festivities?

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: I'm almost certain we didn't. The actors spend the entire party getting ready, and then I usually fall asleep by the end of the play!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The plays are that good are they?

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: I'm an older man. I need a good early night to keep myself going. I am partial to a puzzle though, so solving this crime could keep me up all night!

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: You know, it's a surprisingly sobering moment when someone's murdered beneath your feet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you know him well?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: We were once drinking fellows. The things I've seen him drink without even breaking a sweat...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you drink with him today?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: I don't remember... It seems likely that I would've though. He never liked to prepare for a performance without at least a small drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Assuming you did, where would you have had a drink with him?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Probably down in the cellar. That was always where he preferred to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you admit that you may well have been down there with him today. Do you think something might've happened whilst you were drinking?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: I'd like to think that I would remember something so terrible happening in front of my eyes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And yet you can't actually remember if you'd been drinking with him.

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Oh, that's a good point... In that case, I don't know...

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Quite an unfortunate turn of events, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure it's unfortunate so much as a tragedy surely?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Well, I believe the play was actually intended to be a comedy. I'm hopeful they have an understudy who can fill in for today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The play's still going ahead? Are we just going to ignore that a man was murdered?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: I'd rather not think about it, personally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... So Livius wasn't a customer of yours then?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Would he have reason to have been?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actors seem to have a lot of costumes to move around the place. Maybe a shipping magnate such as yourself would make good money off of them.

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't help you there. Good tip about the actors though. I'll have to visit the College of Bards next time I'm in Tempestus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's probably best if I don't mess around with this again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would Constantinius keep a broken stool around... Unless it was only just broken?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It appears to be a box full of blank wine labels. I suppose they like to keep things well organised.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's got you then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A few cuts and scrapes... No stab wound... Poison perhaps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe there's something nearby that could give me a better idea of what happened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A few cuts and scrapes... No stab wound... Poison perhaps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks like someone's dropped this. I wonder if there was a fight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's only half full. Someone must've been drinking from it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder why this jug's been tucked away in the corner... Was someone trying to hide it?

sections.title: Talking to Hutza or Stradius after investigating everything

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: How's your investigation going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've found everything there is to find so far. What now?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Are you sure you've investigated everything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Do you think you could pin down a suspect?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure. Oddly enough, no one is admitting to having done it, and given they were all here together, I'm pretty sure they'd be each other's alibis.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: What if they hired someone to do it for them?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Stradius... That's actually a good point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe the butlers?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: You mean more of them might be fake like you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks to the masks and uniforms, they're all anonymous. It's also very hard to keep track of how many there are.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Have we been keeping tabs on all the butlers? What if one's escaped! The killer might already be in the wind!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Both of you are missing that there's no window in which they could've left that cellar.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Ah, yes. We came down when we heard the commotion. They wouldn't have been able to get out before then.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: It seems we're back to square one again, [player name], unless you can think of anything else to investigate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could try stealing from the suspects? Perhaps one of them will be carrying something incriminating.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You probably shouldn't mention that in front of us.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: I suppose we can always return things to them later.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Hutza!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be subtle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: No worries. We'll keep everyone under control here while you continue your investigation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not finished yet.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: No worries. We'll keep everyone under control here.

sections.title: Talking to Hutza or Stradius without pickpocketing everyone

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: How is your pocket checking going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Well, keep up the good work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm so clumsy! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder what these are for...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm so clumsy! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm so clumsy! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder what this is about...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm so clumsy! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm so clumsy! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I suppose that's not surprising.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm so clumsy! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder what this is for...

sections.title: Talking to Hutza or Stradius after pickpocketing everyone

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had a little look through some pockets. I think all of the guests might be lying.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: All of them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, maybe not Pavo. They just had this old drinking flask. Xocotla and Cozyac on the other hand both had things on them that revealed they had direct dealings with Livius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, have a look at what I found for yourselves.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Interesting... What about these labels?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found them on Adala.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: She was the one who wasn't accounted for when the murder took place, right?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: That's right. She's the one who came here with Patzi. Like [player name] here, both were uninvited.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Perhaps one of them is responsible... or indeed both of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's possible I suppose, but Patzi doesn't seem capable of pulling this off.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: It seems we still don't have anything conclusive. What do you think we should do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We could get them all together. Put pressure on them with some accusations and see who breaks?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You think that'll work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only one way to find out I suppose.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: It's not the worst thought, and I guess whatever happens, there's a good chance that some more of the truth will come out.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Alright, [player name], just let us know when you want everyone gathered and we'll take them upstairs for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Perfect! Let's get everyone upstairs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to review the evidence a bit more.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Good idea. Just let us know when you're ready.

sections.title: Talking to Hutza or Stradius after giving them the evidence

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: So what are these labels you found?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: You take the lead here, [player name]. We'll be on hand to support you where needed.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: This bit's all up to you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Constantinius, you knew Livius well, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I'd sponsored him for years, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How was that sponsorship working out for you?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Well, I can't say I wasn't profiting from it. Although I do admit that he'd become a little... excessive with his spending in recent times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'd almost benefit from him being out of the way, letting you find someone new to sponsor?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: The thought has crossed my mind, but he was still profitable, and it's not like I couldn't just sponsor a second actor in addition to him anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Constantinius...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Perhaps you should make sure you've questioned everyone before making an accusation, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Constantinius! I believe you were responsible for the murder of Livius. He was getting too expensive to sponsor and you needed him gone!

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I wasn't tied to him... If I'd wanted to stop sponsoring him, I'd have just done that. There would have been no need to kill him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, perhaps you felt a murder mystery would have helped make this party interesting and memorable.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I can't deny that it has very much enhanced the event, but do you think I'd have someone killed just for a social function?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's... a fair point, and indeed, why would you kill your lead actor right before the play?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: You're the one accusing me, [player name]. You should know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Constantinius...

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Haven't you already tried to accuse me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed I have. Just keeping you on your toes. As you were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cozyac, did you not claim to have never interacted with Livius?

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: I did, and I shall stand by that claim! Why are you the one making these accusations anyway? You were the one down there with him! Besides, you're just a butler!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stradius, the note!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A note written by yourself for Livius. Seems you weren't very happy with him...

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: How did you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it seems you did know him. In fact, you had an issue with him, did you not?

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: Not one I would have killed him over! He owed me some money and wasn't paying up. I just needed to put some pressure on him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... An outstanding debt could be considered motive for murder...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cozyac...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Perhaps you should make sure you've questioned everyone before making an accusation, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cozyac! I believe you were responsible for the murder of Livius. He owed you a debt and was refusing to pay!

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: Why? Why kill someone over a debt? A dead man can't repay me, so I'd have needed to actually reclaim the money before killing him, at which point I'd have no reason to kill him anyway!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: He makes a fair point, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cozyac...

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: Haven't you already tried to accuse me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed I have. Just keeping you on your toes. As you were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pavo, you and Livius were drinking partners, weren't you?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: We were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How heavily would you say you drank together?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: On occasion, a little too heavily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who provided the drinks?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: We'd tend to bring our own, although I did sometimes let him have a bit extra from my flask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your flask, huh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stradius, the flask!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This flask?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Yes, that's the one! I must've misplaced it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you were letting Livius drink from your flask, it wouldn't have been hard for you to poison him, would it?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: But why would I poison a friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That remains to be seen...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pavo...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Perhaps you should make sure you've questioned everyone before making an accusation, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pavo! I believe you were responsible for the murder of Livius. You poisoned him when you let him drink from your flask!

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: I would never do something like that! You have no proof!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the flask does still have some liquid left in it. Perhaps we should have it tested?

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Wait, there's still some left?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... Well, I guess it's not poison then. Thanks for... helping to sort that one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pavo...

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Haven't you already tried to accuse me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed I have. Just keeping you on your toes. As you were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xocotla, you've never had any dealings with Livius, have you?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Of course not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he's never needed anything shipped by your company? Nothing he needed to move swiftly and discretely?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Why would he? I'm sure he's got more than enough solutions for moving his costumes around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stradius, the contract!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Care to explain this, Xocotla?

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: That looks like a contract between... myself and Livius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good eye. Interesting how you yourself seemed to not be aware of it, especially given it was you who was carrying it.

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Alright, fine. Livius had recently contracted us to help him move some of his belongings around. He's been doing well for himself and was moving into a larger property.

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: I needed him to sign off on a slight change in timelines, and given we were both here, I thought we may as well get it sorted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xocotla...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Perhaps you should make sure you've questioned everyone before making an accusation, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xocotla! I believe you were responsible for the murder of Livius. You pretended you didn't know him, but you lied!

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Yes... It may surprise you to know that I didn't want to be associated with a murder victim whilst an investigation was ongoing!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: I suppose that is fair, [player name], and lying isn't a crime on its own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xocotla...

lines.speaker: Xocotla
lines.text: Haven't you already tried to accuse me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed I have. Just keeping you on your toes. As you were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Patzi, Adala. The two of you weren't even invited to this party...

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Nor were you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But being here presented a huge opportunity for an up-and-coming actor like Patzi.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: A huge opportunity indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You already knew that Constantinius was thinking about sponsoring a new actor. Perhaps you decided to speed things along a bit by removing the competition.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: But why would I need to remove the competition if he was already open to sponsoring me anyway? That doesn't make sense...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps, and indeed it is true that you couldn't have been present at the time of the murder. You never left the party. In fact you sent me down into the cellar for you, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: So you're saying that you're actually more likely to be guilty yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly!

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sending me down there made me look guilty! A perfect way of drawing attention away from the true culprit.

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: But... that would be a terrible plan. You were here with us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which leads me on to you, Adala. Patzi was accounted for when the murder took place, but you were not. Care to tell us what you were up to?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I took Patzi's bag to the backstage area, spent some time chatting to the actors, and then made my way to the party.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you never entered the wine cellar?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you knew a lot about it, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Where are you going with this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stradius, the labels!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wine labels from Constantinius' cellar, found on your person. Care to explain?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I just found them on the floor. It's bad manners not to pick up litter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What a convenient excuse...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Patzi...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Perhaps you should make sure you've questioned everyone before making an accusation, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Patzi! I believe you were responsible for the murder of Livius. You wanted him out of the way so you could take his place!

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: But why? Constantinius was open to sponsoring me anyway. I didn't need to kill anyone...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: I think he's right, [player name]. There's no real motive here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Adala...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Perhaps you should make sure you've questioned everyone before making an accusation, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Adala! I believe you were responsible for the murder of Livius. All the evidence is pointing at you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were the only person missing at the time of the murder, and despite your claims, we have clear evidence you were in that cellar!

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Alright, fine. I admit I was in the cellar.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Wait, what is this?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: The Principum Red, you fool. I could retire on the value of that wine. All I had to do was get out of here with it.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: My wine!

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: You used me...

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: And I probably would've got away with it too, if it wasn't for this precocious butler turned investigator you hired!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened Adala? Did Livius interrupt your plan? Did he need to be silenced?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Murder! What a ridiculous assertion! I may be a thief, but I'd never kill someone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Patzi...

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Haven't you already tried to accuse me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed I have. Just keeping you on your toes. As you were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I yield! You win!

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I could've killed you, but I'm not that kind of person, just like how I didn't kill Livius.

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: Stop! You're too good for me! You win!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you ready to confess now?

lines.speaker: Adala
lines.text: I promise I didn't do it! I'm just a thief! I wasn't even strong enough to defeat you. How could I have killed Livius?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: She may be telling the truth, [player name], but either way, we know she's a criminal now.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Come with us, Adala. You can have fun contemplating your crimes from your new cell...

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Does this mean we can get on with tonight's play?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You still want to go ahead with the play? You don't even have a lead actor anymore!

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Yes, it is a bit of a last minute issue, but I have no doubt that Livius' understudy will be able to step up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Adala might not have even been the killer!

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Well, you've been doing a wonderful job so far, so I'm sure if the villain is still loose, you'll catch them.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Constantinius, with respect, I'm not sure we can let everyone leave until we're sure we have this case solved.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: That's not a problem. You can easily keep everyone in the theatre. The only place they'd be able to run to from there is straight off the cliff!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Constantinius...

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: It's settled then! We will proceed with the play! While we do that, if the killer is still at large, the ever capable [player name] here will ensure they are dealt with!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, let's get going then.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Oh, no, you can't come with us. I don't want your investigation disrupting things. You'll need to stay out of sight. Why don't you take the back way?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The back way?

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Yes! It's not been used in a while, so it's a little bit precarious these days, but I'm sure you'll manage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. Where do I find this back way?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The pathway leads around the cliffs to the south. We'll see you at the theatre, [player name].

sections.title: Attempting to climb up the loose rocks before being asked to

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I should be going up there.

sections.title: Attempting to enter through the backstage entrance before talking to Hutza or Stradius

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no reason to go down there right now.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's the plan here?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Well... we don't really have one, but the play is about to start and we still might have a killer on the loose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps there's some evidence we missed somewhere.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: It seemed like you'd been quite thorough. The only place you didn't check was here at the theatre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then I guess it's time to fix that.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: The stairs over there lead down to the backstage area. That's where the actors, including Livius, were getting ready for the play. That seems like the best place to check for any missing clues.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to it.

sections.title: Talking to Hutza or Stradius without investigating the backstage

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Any luck down in the backstage area?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Keep at it! I'm sure you can solve this one!

sections.title: Searching the crate before investigating other clues

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Nilsal. Are you the one who's trying to find Livius' killer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. So I'm guessing down here is your domain?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: If you think I have any actual control in here, you'd be sorely mistaken. I don't think anyone could keep these actors in line. They're a... dramatic bunch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speaking of which, where are they?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: The play will be starting as we speak. They'll all be up in the theatre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, well in that case, I'd better get to it. I'd like to have a look around down here. Hopefully I can find some sort of clue as to how Livius died.

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd also like to ask you a few questions, if that's okay?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the actors?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: There's four of them in total... Well... was four I should say.

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: There's Naiatli. She was Livius' understudy. After what happened, she's now taking the lead role tonight.

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Then you have Clodius. He's getting close to retirement now so prefers to take on small, but impactful roles.

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Finally there's Quintilia. She's quite new, so she mostly takes on the non-speaking parts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And all of them were in here earlier?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: That's right. Livius went out for his pre-performance drink, as he likes to do. The others all stayed here until after the murder happened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you're sure none of them could have slipped out at any point?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: I don't think so. I suppose I do get quite engrossed in my work sometimes, so it's not impossible. But I'm sure I'd have spotted someone going up the stairs...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the crate of poison for?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Ah, that was a gift from Constantinius. We were having a bit of a rat problem, so he gave us the poison to deal with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems one of the costumes has a stain on it.

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: A stain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, a wine stain. Apparently the costume belongs to Naiatli. Is there any reason the costume would be stained?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: No, none of her characters have any interaction with wine...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any wine backstage?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Well, the actors do often like to have a drink after a play, but I always ask that they do that outside so as to not risk any damage to the costumes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you know there was a hidden passage in here?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: A passage? I had no idea, but I suppose it's not too suprising.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: This is an old villa. Apparently it once played a role in some smuggling operations a long time ago. I could see them having a few secrets to help them with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to keep looking around.

sections.title: Searching the crate after investigating other clues
Talking to Hutza or Stradius after investigating the backstage

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The play is well underway and for all we know there's still a killer out there. I hope you have something for us, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I might. It turns out there's a secret passage from the backstage area into the wine cellar. Someone could have got in and out without anyone else knowing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not only that, but the backstage area has had a rat problem recently. They have plenty of poison for dealing with them. That same poison could easily have been used to kill Livius.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Great work, [player name].

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: But did you find anything that could tell us who did it?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Yes, we need to confront the killer! Who was it, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Patzi.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: But how? We established that he couldn't have accessed the cellar.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: That's right. The secret passage doesn't change that either as he never left the villa.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, not him then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Adala.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: So it was her? But why?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Adala may be a criminal, but she had no reason to want Livius dead. I don't think it was her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Good point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Constantinius.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: How would he have gained from killing Livius?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Exactly. If he's wanted to stop sponsoring him, he could have just done that. No killing needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you're right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cozyac.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Because of the debt? That could be a motive, but he never had the means.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I suppose you're right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xocotla.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: She did lie about knowing him, but we never determined any proper motive.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I don't think she ever had the opportunity either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you're right actually. Ignore me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pavo.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I thought we'd ruled them out?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Do you have new information that changes that, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... No.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Well then it's probably not them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Naiatli.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The understudy that's on stage right now? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As a fellow actor, she had access to the secret passage into the wine cellar where Livius was having his pre- performance drink.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: But how did she kill him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The rat poison. Enough of that would easily kill a man. She poisoned his drink so that she could be back out of the cellar before he died.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I suppose that makes sense, but she wasn't the only one who had access to that passage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But she was the only one who had wine on her clothes. Wine isn't allowed backstage, so it must have come from the cellar.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Oh, very good! But what about motive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Constantinius mentioned earlier that she had been in Livius' shadow for some time. Killing him has finally allowed her to take on a leading role.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I suppose this does all make sense, but are you certain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd say eighty, maybe ninety percent sure.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Well I suppose that will have to be enough. You've not taken us down too many wrong paths today...

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Except for the whole fake butler act.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: True, but you were honest about that pretty quickly. We'll wait until the play is over and then we'll take her in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait? But should we not be acting now?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Constantinius would kill us if we stopped the performance.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: And then we'd have another murder mystery on our hands...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then we'll have to just speed things along a bit. I'm going out there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Clodius.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The actor? But why would he do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, he was one of the few who had access to the secret passage into the wine cellar. He also had access to the rat poison.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: But what was his motive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I don't know.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: We're going to need more than that, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quintilia.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The actress? But why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She had access to both the secret passage into the wine cellar and to the rat poison.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: But she doesn't have a motive... She also isn't the only one who had access to the passage and poison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... Good point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Costumer.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, she had access to the secret passage. She claimed not to know about it, but she did know about the history of the villa. She also had access to the rat poison.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: But why would she kill him? What's the motive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... don't know.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Sounds like she might not be our killer then, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Livius.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You think Livius killed himself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it's the last thing anyone would expect.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: It's the last thing anyone would expect because it obviously didn't happen! Let's try considering some more plausible candidates.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You two.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Us? But surely we'd remember...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Of course it wasn't us! Stop messing around, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: You?! Goodness, it's the crime of the century! Right, come with us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! I was joking!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Stop playing around, [player name]! We have a killer to deal with!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The dog.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: The dog? But how would a dog do something like this?

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Never underestimate a dog, Hutza.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Okay, but even if the dog did have the means, it didn't have the opportunity, not to mention motive...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, fine, not the dog.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know yet. I need to review the evidence.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Be quick, [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Hutza or Stradius again

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: We need to confront the killer! Who was it, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Did you want to leave?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'm going on stage.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Break a leg out there!

sections.title: Subsequently talking to Hutza or Stradius

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need go back on stage.

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: Go and get her!

sections.title: Attempting to go through the guest entryway

lines.speaker: Villa Guard
lines.text: Shouldn't you be on stage?

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: At last, the vile villain shows themselves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Villain? The only villain here is you, murderer!

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: I am no murderer! What vexatious allegations you come at me with!

lines.speaker: Cozyac
lines.text: I'm not sure about this new character. Are they even in costume?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do not pretend you don't know! Your lies are at an end!

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I don't think this was in the original script...

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: I've overcome so much! I won't fall at this last hurdle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lay down your arms! Your heroic deeds are fabrications!

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: Never! Now swing at me!

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: Argh! You're stronger than I thought... I need to regroup!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Face me!

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: Ha! You've stepped into my trap.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A trap?

lines.speaker: Clodius
lines.text: Aha, villain! This is where you fall!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're no match for me!

lines.speaker: Clodius
lines.text: I'm sorry... You'll have to fight alone.

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: How could you be this powerful?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all part of my grand plan. I grow stronger with every passing moment!

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: I can't keep up with this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then flee again, ignoble hero!

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: I must make my last stand... Could you find it in your heart to stay your hand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And let you live to foil my plans another day? I'm afraid that's not the game we play!

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: And so I take my leave from this mortal coil!

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Bravo!

lines.speaker: Pavo
lines.text: Well that was a twist, wasn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Naiatli, I know you killed Livius. Why?

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: I've been stuck in the shadow of that man for too long. He wouldn't even let me fill another role...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Murder is a bit far though. Could you not have just... lightly poisoned him?

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: You don't understand... I never poisoned him... I just tripped him. All I wanted was to put him out of action. A small injury would've been enough for him to not perform this evening...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Naiatli
lines.text: He'd had so much to drink. He fell harder than expected, and he wouldn't get up... I fled... I never wanted him to...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, Naiatli... Come on, let's get you to the guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stradius, Hutza, it's been a surprising pleasure working with you to solve this case.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: I can't believe it was all an accident...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: [player name], you've done well. I'd like to apologise for our hasty arrest earlier. Your commitment to helping us has been commendable.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: To be fair to us, you did seem like the perfect suspect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I did, but no harm done. I'm just glad we got it all sorted out, though I don't think Patzi is going to be paying me any time soon...

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: You win some and you lose some I guess. All the best, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Hutza
lines.text: You were quite impressive up on stage. Maybe you should take up a career in acting... or investigating crime.

lines.speaker: Stradius
lines.text: Thanks for your help again, [player name]. It's good to know people like you are around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still here, Patzi?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: No thanks to you! This was meant to be a big moment for me, and you totally upstaged me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you angry at me for getting involved in all of this? Did you forget that it was you that hired me?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I hired you to make me look better!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I don't think I did too bad a job. You never got kicked out, even though you did bring a thief in with you.

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how about that payment you promised?

lines.speaker: Patzi
lines.text: Ah... Well... You see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I thought as much.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Well, I never imagined you'd be quite the actor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to admit, I rather surprised myself there.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: I don't suppose you'd like to be acting here in the future? To be honest, I'd prefer you to that odd fellow that brought you to my door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a kind offer, but I'm afraid it was a one-time job.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: A shame to be sure.

lines.speaker: Constantinius
lines.text: Anyway, do feel free to wander around the villa whenever you fancy. I don't recommend drinking from the wine fountain though.

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: You were very brave for going on stage to confront a killer. Looks like you did well to get her to surrender without damaging her costume.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was very well made. Makes the job of not damaging it much easier!

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: Why thank you! I credit my needles for the good work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would I be able to get one of them for my own purposes?

lines.speaker: Costumer
lines.text: I suppose I could part with one... If you're careful not to lose it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be careful.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: After all that, are you sure I can't convince you to join the union?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I was wondering if...

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Kuani! I'll need to run and grab my forms, but if you wait there just a moment...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was going to ask if you had any spare equipment...

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Spare equipment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may have a need to mask my identity again in the future... or perhaps carry a tray around.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: You're certain you don't want to join the union as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: I see. In that case, which equipment would you like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The ram mask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The wolf mask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The bird mask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The jaguar mask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The snake mask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The butler's tray.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: None of them.

lines.speaker: Head Butler
lines.text: Very well.

sections.title: Pickpocketing Constantinius while already having the key

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder what this opens...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *Gulp*
